MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC's motion for summary adjudication is granted.

Summary adjudication of Plaintiffs' claim for premises owner/contractor liability is GRANTED.
Plaintiffs' special interrogatory response asserts that Defendant Syd Carpenter Marine Contractor, Inc. installed asbestos-containing material including asbestos-containing insulation material, supplied by Metalclad. (Huynh Decl. Ex. D at 4-5.) Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence sufficient to create a triable issue of fact establishing Metalclad's ownership, possession, or control of property alleged to be in a dangerous or defective condition. (Lopez v. Superior Court (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 705, 7014.)

Summary adjudication of Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is also GRANTED. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Plaintiffs' special interrogatory response demonstrates that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Huynh Decl. Ex. D at 7-13.) Plaintiffs' identification of workers compensation claims is insufficient to establish that Metalclad was on notice that asbestos was hazardous by the time Plaintiffs was exposed. (Id. at 8.) Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' reliance on its interrogatory responses as evidence is disallowed under CCP 437c(p)(2). Defendant's objections to Gold Decl. Exhibits 2-4 are sustained as irrelevant.

While Plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find Plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING: MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A) AND (D)
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted and amended as follows: Plaintiff's motion to specially set a trial date is granted under C.C.P. 36(a) and C.C.P 36(d). Trial date is October 29, 2018 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is December 13, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 25, 2018. The parties stipulate that summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on 25 days notice. Opposition due 9 court days before the hearing. Reply due 5 court days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Plaintiff shall respond to written discovery 14 days. Any previously served discovery shall be responded to within 14 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is sooner. Defendant shall respond to written discovery in 21 days. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than September 14, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 28, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via by October 19, 2018 at 2:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with is set for October 25, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 603.
